timeline.label: Closed Apellis acquisition (~$5.3B cash plus CVRs; $2B term debt drawn) to add SYFOVRE/EMPAVELI and geographic complement.
Filed FY2025 10-K: $9.9B revenue, ~$2.1B free cash flow, MS erosion offset by growth products +19%.
TECFIDERA relevant European patent revoked; management expects continued revenue decline.
FDA approval of LEQEMBI (lecanemab) for Alzheimer's; collaboration economics with Eisai.
FDA approval of QALSODY (tofersen) for SOD1-ALS; rare-disease growth pillar.
FDA accelerated approval of ADUHELM (aducanumab); later commercial retrenchment.
FDA approval of VUMERITY (diroximel fumarate) for MS; oral franchise extension.
FDA approval of SPINRAZA (nusinersen) for SMA; foundational rare-disease cash flow.
FDA approval of TECFIDERA (dimethyl fumarate); became largest MS product before generic erosion.
FDA approval of TYSABRI return to market with REMS; high-efficacy MS anchor.

industry.structure: Global neuroscience biopharma with concentrated MS and rare-disease franchises; high R&D intensity, patent cliffs, and payer/rebate pressure on mature products.
industry.share_shift: Biogen is losing MS share to generics and competing high-efficacy agents while gaining in rare disease (SPINRAZA, SKYCLARYS) and attempting Alzheimer's (LEQEMBI) and geographic expansion via Apellis.
industry.trend: Industry pivot toward immunology and rare disease; Alzheimer's antibodies scaling slowly; large M&A (Apellis) increases leverage and CVR-linked contingent payoffs.
